flows.name: New project onboarding flow
flows.trigger: Developer runs 'devforge init' in a new or existing project directory
stages: 1. devforge init scans directory for WORKSPACE_PROJECT_MARKERS (pyproject.toml, package.json, etc.)
2. Interactive wizard collects LLM mode (auto/offline/live), knowledge focus (balanced/implementation/testing), context size (standard/lean/rich)
3. Wizard maps human-readable choices to llm_preferences, knowledge_preferences, pull_policy_overrides
4. Writes .devforge/devforge.snapshot.json with initiative + project + empty work_packages structure
5. Writes .devforge/devforge.project_config.json with wizard-derived preferences
6. Developer runs 'devforge snapshot .devforge/devforge.snapshot.json' to execute first cycle
7. run_cycle dispatches work packages, returns updated snapshot with status changes
8. Developer writes back updated snapshot for subsequent cycles
flows.termination: First cycle completes with at least one work package in 'completed' or 'verified' status; or all work packages exhaust retries and reach 'failed' status
evidence: tests/test_main_cli.py — init and snapshot command integration tests

flows.name: Existing repo onboarding flow
flows.trigger: Snapshot contains a project with kind='existing_repo' and work package 'wp-repo-onboarding' in 'ready' status
stages: 1. run_cycle selects wp-repo-onboarding from workset (existing_repo kind triggers onboarding path)
2. Dispatcher routes wp-repo-onboarding to primary executor (codex) with repository context payload
3. Executor analyzes repository structure and writes findings to handoff_notes
4. If wp-repo-onboarding reaches 'verified': _maybe_seed_follow_up_work() injects wp-initial-work-plan
5. Next cycle selects wp-initial-work-plan (analysis_design phase, execution_planner role)
6. Planner converts onboarding findings into concrete next-iteration backlog
7. If wp-initial-work-plan verified: _maybe_seed_existing_repo_implementation_work() injects three implementation work packages
8. Implementation cycles execute: wp-runtime-state-persistence, wp-executor-live-adapters, wp-onboarding-entry-flow
9. After all implementation verified: _maybe_seed_existing_repo_validation_work() injects testing + acceptance packages
flows.termination: wp-self-hosting-acceptance reaches 'verified' status; or all executors exhausted and python local_runner closes out with acceptance report

flows.name: Failed executor recovery flow
flows.trigger: A work package executor returns a non-zero exit code or is missing from PATH; attempt_count increments toward max_attempts
stages: 1. Executor transport returns failed SubmissionReceipt or subprocess non-zero exit
2. run_cycle calls decide_retry_action(work_package) — rule-based guardrail first
3. resolve_retry_action() checks attempt_count vs max_attempts and executor availability
4. If rule guardrail passes: llm_retry_decider() routes to one of: switch_executor / requeue / block / replan / final_fail
5. switch_executor: work package reassigned to fallback_executors[0], status reset to 'ready'
6. requeue: work package status reset to 'ready' with same executor for retry
7. block: work package set to 'blocked', cycle continues with remaining work
8. replan: replan_reason set on RuntimeState, triggers planning_and_shaping node in next cycle
9. final_fail: work package marked 'failed' with retry_action='final_fail'
10. For existing_repo projects: _maybe_seed_failed_onboarding_acceptance() injects python local_runner acceptance closeout WP
flows.termination: Work package reaches terminal status (failed/blocked/completed via fallback) OR local acceptance closeout package is executed and produces bootstrap report
key_artifacts: work_package.handoff_notes — executor error messages and partial findings
work_package.retry_action — final routing decision recorded on WP

flows.name: Multi-cycle continuation flow
flows.trigger: Developer runs 'devforge snapshot' a second time with the updated snapshot from a previous cycle result
stages: 1. Previous cycle result['snapshot'] written back to .devforge/devforge.snapshot.json
2. run_snapshot_cycle loads updated snapshot (work packages carry status from previous cycle)
3. run_cycle calls _requirement_events_from_snapshot — processes any pending requirement patches first
4. select_workset() filters for 'ready' work packages respecting depends_on order
5. If seeding logic triggers (based on previous WP status): new work packages injected into snapshot
6. LLM planning decider confirms or reorders workset selection
7. Dispatches selected work packages with context carry-over from previous cycle memory
8. Persistence layer appends events to JSONL/SQLite — full audit trail of multi-cycle progression
9. Updated snapshot returned with new cycle_id
flows.termination: Cycle terminates when: termination_signal set (acceptance passed), no runnable work remains, or user interrupts via REPL
key_artifacts: result['events'] — in-memory event list for single-cycle inspection

flows.name: Project config override flow
flows.trigger: Developer provides --project-config flag or a sibling .project_config.json file exists alongside the snapshot
stages: 1. load_project_config() reads devforge.project_config.json
2. apply_project_config() iterates projects in snapshot, finds matching project_id keys in config
3. pull_policy_overrides: normalize_pull_policy_overrides() validates and normalizes rules, writes back to project
4. llm_preferences: merged into project.llm_preferences dict
5. knowledge_preferences: merged into project.knowledge_preferences dict
6. Merged snapshot passed to run_cycle()
7. select_knowledge_for_context() reads knowledge_preferences.preferred_ids + excluded_ids
8. resolve_pull_strategy() reads pull_policy_overrides to select context mode/budget
9. build_task_llm_client() reads llm_preferences to select provider/model
10. Dispatches use overridden context and LLM routing
flows.termination: Cycle completes with preferences applied. Config is stateless — not persisted back to snapshot. Re-apply on each cycle.
key_artifacts: result['runtime']['selected_knowledge'] — confirms knowledge selection was applied
evidence: tests/test_graph_runner.py:29-78 — verifies executor_payload shape includes applied preferences

flows.name: Multi-project workspace + seam governance flow
flows.trigger: apply_project_split() called with source project, child project defs, and seam definition (e.g., triggered by planning decision during analysis_design phase)
stages: 1. apply_project_split() creates child projects in snapshot, assigns work packages to children
2. Source project status set to 'split_done', coordination_project = True
3. SeamState created between child projects with status='draft'
4. Technical architect work package dispatched to define seam contract
5. freeze_seam() called after seam contract accepted — status transitions to 'frozen'
6. Implementation work packages dispatched in parallel across child projects
7. verify_seam_compliance() called on implementation outputs against frozen SeamState
8. If deviation detected: SeamRisk raised, seam status set to 'broken'
9. Broken seam triggers retry/replan for affected work packages
10. Integration verification closes seam with status='verified'
flows.termination: All seams reach 'verified' status; or broken seam triggers replan and new implementation iteration
key_artifacts: SeamState JSON in snapshot.seams[] — contract definition with acceptance_criteria
work_package.related_seams — links WPs to their governing seams
SeamRisk objects — deviation evidence from verify_seam_compliance()
evidence: tests/test_e2e_game.py:23-44 — project split and seam creation test
tests/test_e2e_game.py:51-80 — seam freeze and break detection test

flows.name: Acceptance and gap remediation flow
flows.trigger: All work packages in the current workset reach 'completed' status; acceptance_and_gap_check_node triggered
stages: 1. acceptance_and_gap_check_node() evaluates work package results against acceptance_goals
2. evaluate_acceptance() LLM call: checks goal completion, closure density, result quality
3. If acceptance passes: termination_signal = True, cycle terminates cleanly
4. If acceptance fails: replan_reason set (e.g., 'acceptance_failed'), termination_signal not set
5. analyze_gaps() LLM call: attributes gaps to specific work package failures or missing coverage
6. analyze_gaps() returns RemediationPackage with new work package proposals
7. apply_requirement_events() or direct snapshot mutation injects remediation work packages
8. Next cycle selects remediation work packages and dispatches
9. Convergence loop repeats until acceptance passes or max_attempts exhausted
flows.termination: termination_signal = True (acceptance passed) OR all remediation paths exhausted with final_fail
key_artifacts: AcceptanceVerdict dataclass — goal check results and overall verdict
RemediationPackage — new work package proposals from gap analysis
